items.q: What does a curly arrow in an organic mechanism represent?
items.correct: The movement of a pair of electrons from where they are to where they end up.
items.misconception: A curly arrow shows the movement of an atom from one position to another.
items.random_wrong: A curly arrow shows the direction in which heat is flowing in the reaction.
items.why: Curly arrows look like they're pointing to atoms, so students think they describe atomic motion rather than electron pair movement.

items.q: Why is the hydration enthalpy of a small, highly charged ion very exothermic?
items.correct: Small/highly charged ions form strong ion-dipole attractions with water molecules, releasing a large amount of energy.
items.misconception: Hydration enthalpy only depends on the size of the ion, not its charge.
items.random_wrong: Hydration enthalpy is the same for all ions because water molecules are identical.
items.why: Students focus on one factor (size OR charge) when actually both matter — charge density determines the strength of ion-dipole interactions.

items.q: If 50 cm^3 of a 1 mol/dm^3 solution is diluted to 100 cm^3, what is the new concentration?
items.correct: 0.5 mol/dm^3 — the moles stay the same but the volume doubled.
items.misconception: The new concentration is 0.05 mol/dm^3 because you doubled the volume so you halve the concentration once and shift the decimal too.
items.random_wrong: The new concentration is 2 mol/dm^3 because adding water makes the solution stronger.
items.why: Students panic with unit conversions and over-divide, or they confuse 'add water = more concentrated' through a misread of the procedure.

items.q: Sodium has atomic number 11. Its electron configuration is:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 2,8,1. “2,9” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: An atom of Mg loses 2 electrons to form Mg²⁺. Its number of protons is:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 12. “10” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many neutrons in an atom with mass number 23 and atomic number 11?
items.why: Exact answer: 12 neutrons. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
